PAIN.quote: I had extreme case of bad slice with driver. It was so bad that if I swing as hard as I can without worrying about anything, I could probably send the ball 2 fairways to the right
as most of you slice-happy users know, its NOT THAT EASY!
I know how frustrating it is
Nothing has worked and now I'm just frustrated
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: I go to the range and tinker all day long and nothing works consistently
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: I still sent 70%+ of my drives with either a fade or it starts right and slices hard right
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: I've tried everything in my power to shallow the club out
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: The title says it all. And it isn't the specific club, I am not trying to come up with a reason to buy a new one. I just can't hit it
All I do is hit big pull slices with my driver. I can tee off with my 5w and 3w just fine
the driver is a complete crap shoot
I guess this is just a rant, I am very upset with myself
I'm experiencing the same thing. I hit my driver on trackman indoors... goes fine, feels fine. Get outside... suddenly I can't hit it
My current driver is a Nike Ignite and it's the only thing in my bag I can't hit. With all my other clubs, it's either middle to middle right. With driver it's in the adjacent fairway
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can draw-bias driver fix slice

PAIN.quote: I know a guy that has his driver set to close and the weights set in a draw bias and it does nothing to stop his slice
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can draw-bias driver fix slice

PAIN.quote: hitting the driver well is the biggest struggle of my game right now. Depending on the course I may have 1/2 dozen punch out shots from bad drives
I have many many many...many drivers varying in all types all lengths. You name it & I probably have it or had it. Still looking for the 'One'
My big fear on the golf course is the right side, like most people I started playing with a slice. And while that has gotten leaps and bounds better with all my other clubs, I can't hit the driver
I think the fear of the right side gets me so tense that the only swing I have is my original hack swing
PAIN.context: Fear causing tension and worse swings

PAIN.quote: It's terrible. One of the worst feeling golf clubs I've ever hit, feels like hitting a brick. Can't even hit it as far as my hybrid
It feels like hitting a rock, and it doesn't go much farther than a standard hybrid
PAIN.source: Reddit - Is GX7 actually a scrub club

HOPE.quote: Get a DRAW biased / HT(high trajectory, high loft) driver... HUGE difference. HUGGGE difference
HOPE.context: Strong endorsement of draw bias as solution

HOPE.quote: If you are actively looking to correct your slice, having a consistent driver will help
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

HOPE.quote: If you can be 30 or so yards closer to the center and only a few yards shorter, you could save a lot of strokes
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

HOPE.quote: The Callaway Paradym Ai Smoke Max D was among the best drivers we tested for keeping the ball on the left side of the golf course
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

HOPE.quote: A draw driver will straighten out your drives and let you hit them farther since you will not be losing distance to your slice. You will love this for about 2 months
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver

HOPE.quote: I just feel that If I could get off the tee with my driver, my confidence would go up and I could get to single digits
The one fairway I hit led to a birdie
If driver is only club you have no confidence in, it's time for a new one... Have to love your driver and be confident with it
Many draw-biased drivers struggle with distance performance. The Paradym Ai Smoke Max D stood out with a distance score of 9.4/10
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

HOPE.quote: if you hit the occasional slice, along with the occasional hook with some straight shots mixed in, turning to the SF1 driver is probably not the answer. But if you have a consistent slice that's making golf less fun, this driver really could help
my first couple swings with the SF1 driver, just swinging it with my normal setup and swing... each traveled more than 50 yards in the draw/hook direction

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: If you have a pull slice, like I used to, it's from swinging out an outside in path. The ball starts left (due to the path of the club coming right to left), and spins back hard right due to the high amount of spin
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Can draw-bias driver fix slice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: If you have a pull slice, like I did, a closed faced driver can make the problem even worse. This is what happened with me
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Can draw-bias driver fix slice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I think, long term, it makes it worse by allowing you to get away with it instead of fixing it
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Can draw-bias driver fix slice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The longer the club, the more it amplifies any swing faults you have
since it's also the longest (shafted) club and we're expected to hit the ball the farthest with it, it's easy to tense up with it, let the swing get too long, put a violent, bad swing on it
as the driver is such a longer club it's easy to have too much body and not enough arms, so get a bit OTT or draggy with the handle
What length driver are you playing? Most off-the-shelf drivers are 45-46" so manufacturers can get max distance now that COR is maxed out
I'm 6'3" and cut my driver to 44", finding many more fairways now due to more consistent face angle and contact spot
MOI is just a number. Drivers with high MOI tend to offer better ball speed on off-center hits but they are not necessarily built to help you hit straighter shots
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: moveable weights sometimes cause more problems than they solve. The movable weights reduce spin and, while a reduction in spin can help straighten out your slice, it can create other issues
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I already got with a instructor, he changed my grip and closed my front shoulder... Still nothing consistent even after multiple range trips and rounds
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: That's why I'm confused. Felt like I was fed a bunch of nonsense
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I have had a few lessons this year. My instructor wants me to line up slightly right and try to hit a push draw. When on the launch monitor I can pull this off and my miss is just a push, to push fade that isn't horrible. But in practice I hit big push slices doing this
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Lessons work on monitor, not on course

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I have a shaft that plays 44" and the stock shaft that plays 45 3/4". Doesn't matter which one I use the result is the same. I even experimented with a playing length of 41"
I suffered from a driver slice for my first two years and I made two changes and now only hit one or so a round... I strengthened my grip and closed my stance about 5 degrees
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: If you straighten out your swing the draw driver will be unhittable, and it will punish 'good' swings
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I would stay away from the Draw drivers. They are very closed, and you won't ever improve your swing
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: start out with a 2-3 closed driver, move to a 1-2 degree closed, to a neutral, to an open driver... Plan on it taking 2 years to groove your swing
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Remember, this is JUST AN AID, not the solution
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Draw driver as aid, not cure

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: A driver will not fix you. If you want a band aid fix try closing the face of the driver at address
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Can draw-bias driver fix slice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: If you have a right-miss tendency off the tee (RH golfers), a CG-shifting backweight that's moveable into Draw and Draw+ settings provides up to 20 yards of right-to-left shot correction

Straight Flight Technology - New to the SFT head is a moveable, 22-gram tungsten backweight that positions the CG to deliver 13 yards of right-to-left shot correction in the Draw setting, and another 7 yards in the Draw+ (20 yards total)
The built-in draw bias will help you minimize side spin and close the face a little faster
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Higher-lofted drivers can help promote a higher, straighter launch. Slicers should look for drivers with a slightly higher loft in the 10.5- to 12-degree range
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Smaller head, extreme heel/toe and back weighting, Hot face, higher loft and reduced turf interaction. Looks like it would work well for their target customer which is the senior/am with lower SS

The crown of the SF1 driver is what Performance Golf calls an AeroSquare crown... visually, if you're struggling with a slice, it's confidence-inspiring. The design cuts through the crown at about a 45-degree angle
If you were to close your eyes and set up in your driver stance with the SF1 driver, then open your eyes and look down, you'd notice that the face appears to be somewhat closed
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: SF1 closed face at address appearance

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: the Mizuno ST-G helped provide a more gradual approach to spin reduction. It lets you dial in your shot shape correction without creating other issues with a lack of spin
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Rogue ST MAX D is built for a high level of draw bias, with high MOI, a more upright lie, reduced face progression
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Callaway Rogue ST Max D thread

VILLAIN.quote: If you are fine with buying your game, then do it and enjoy the results
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver
VILLAIN.context: Draw driver as 'buying your game' criticism

VILLAIN.quote: After that, you will realize that you are not hitting those drives, it is the driver letting your slice swing hit it straighter
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver

VILLAIN.quote: Most reviews I see on it say it's basically a budget 3W. Slightly bigger, fairly forgiving for what it is, but doesn't perform quite as good as most name brand
Is draw bias in a driver enough to fix an extreme slice? Probably not
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

VILLAIN.quote: Through testing, we have found that MOI is just a number
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice

GolfWRX - Can draw-bias driver fix slice
GolfWRX - Should I get draw driver
MyGolfSpy - Drivers that help with slice
high_value_themes_identified: Fear of right side causing tension - psychological PAIN
Monitor vs course performance gap - PAIN
Draw bias as aid not cure - nuanced HOPE
20 yards correction from Ping SFT - quantified COMPETITOR_MECHANISM
MOI is just a number - ROOT_CAUSE debunking
